question: On July 15, 2008, Phys.org published an article about a catastrophe. Find the explosive force of this catastrophe according to Encyclopedia Britannica, then find the name of the US nuclear test that had the same yield. Your answer should only be the last word of the name of the test.

research_queries: Tunguska explosion explosive force Encyclopedia Britannica megatons
US nuclear tests yields list megatons
nuclear test same yield Tunguska explosion
US atomic bomb tests 15 megaton yield
working_plan: Search for the specific Phys.org article published on July 15, 2008 about a catastrophe
Identify what catastrophe the article discusses (likely Tunguska explosion)
Look up the explosive force/yield of that catastrophe in Encyclopedia Britannica
Research US nuclear tests to find one with the same explosive yield
Extract only the last word from the matching nuclear test's name

solver.reasoning_summary: Found the Phys.org article from July 15, 2008 about the Tunguska catastrophe. Encyclopedia Britannica states the Tunguska explosion energy was equivalent to 'as much as 15 megatons of TNT'. Castle Bravo was the US nuclear test with exactly 15 megatons yield. The question asks for only the last word of the test name, which is 'Bravo'.

Tunguska catastrophe: Evidence of acid rain
supports meteorite theory
July 15 2008
  
 
  
Evgeniy Kolesnikov photographed the same place 60 years later. The fallen
trunks are still there, with the taiga growing in between them. Photo: Evgeniy M.
Kolesnikov, Lomonosov Moscow State University
(PhysOrg.com) -- The Tunguska catastrophe in 1908 evidently led to
high levels of acid rain. This is the conclusion reached by Russian,
Italian and German researchers based on the results of analyses of peat
profiles taken from the disaster region.

Annotator Metadata.Steps:
1. Search for "phys org archive"
2. Click on the link for https://phys.org/archive
3. Naviage to July 15, 2008
4. Search the articles for an article that mentions "catastrophe"
5. Note the name of the event (Tunguska catastrophe)
6. Search for "Tunguska catastrophe britannica"
7. Click on the link for Tunguska event
8. Locate the explosive force in the article (15 megatons)
9. Search for "us nuclear test 15 megatons"
10. Record the last word of the name of the test in the search results.
